docs.title: Pattrie
docs.text:
Fast IP prefix trie for Python with a Rust backend. Supports longest-prefix match (LPM) lookups on IPv4 and IPv6 networks.

Built with PyO3 and the prefix-trie crate.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install pattrie

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
import pattrie

t = pattrie.Pattrie()
t["10.0.0.0/8"] = "rfc1918"
t["10.1.0.0/16"] = "internal"

t["10.1.2.3"]          # "internal"  (longest-prefix match)
t["10.2.0.1"]          # "rfc1918"
"10.1.2.3" in t        # True
t.get_key("10.1.2.3")  # "10.1.0.0/16"
t.get("192.0.2.1")     # None

Keys can be strings, ipaddress.IPv4Address, IPv4Network, IPv6Address, or IPv6Network.

docs.title: IPv6
docs.text:
import socket
import pattrie

t = pattrie.Pattrie(128, socket.AF_INET6)
t["2001:db8::/32"] = "documentation"
t["fe80::/10"] = "link-local"

t["fe80::1"]  # "link-local"

docs.title: Concurrent Reads with freeze()
docs.text:
After freeze(), read operations release the GIL, allowing true parallel lookups from multiple threads:

t = pattrie.Pattrie()
# ... populate ...
t.freeze()  # trie becomes read-only; reads release the GIL

# Safe to call from many threads simultaneously
value = t["10.1.2.3"]

t.thaw()    # restore mutability

docs.title: Cross-Process Sharing with dump() / load()
docs.text:
Serialize a frozen trie to a binary file and load it back in another process:

# Writer process
t = pattrie.Pattrie()
t["10.0.0.0/8"] = "rfc1918"
t.freeze()
t.dump("routes.ptri")

# Reader process
t2 = pattrie.Pattrie.load("routes.ptri")
t2["10.1.2.3"]  # "rfc1918"

docs.title: pattrie.Pattrie
docs.text:
A longest-prefix-match (LPM) IP prefix trie.

Stores arbitrary Python values keyed by IP prefixes. Lookups return the value associated with the longest matching prefix.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default maxbits

Maximum prefix length accepted (1–32 for IPv4, 1–128 for IPv6). Defaults to 32.

required family

Address family — socket.AF_INET (default) or socket.AF_INET6.

required

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If family is not AF_INET or AF_INET6, or if maxbits is out of range for the chosen family.

Example 
import pattrie

t = pattrie.Pattrie()
t["10.0.0.0/8"] = "rfc1918"
t["10.1.0.0/16"] = "internal"

t["10.1.2.3"]  # → "internal"  (longest match wins)
t["10.2.0.1"]  # → "rfc1918"

docs.title: __setitem__(key: NetworkKey, value: object) -> None
docs.text:
Insert or replace a prefix using t[prefix] = value.

Equivalent to insert(prefix, value). Host bits are zeroed before insertion ("10.1.2.3/8" is stored as "10.0.0.0/8").

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the trie is frozen or the prefix is invalid.

docs.title: __getitem__(key: AddressKey) -> object
docs.text:
Longest-prefix-match lookup using t[key].

Returns the value stored for the longest matching prefix.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default key AddressKey

An IP address or network prefix (string, IPv4Address, IPv6Address, IPv4Network, or IPv6Network).

required

Returns:

Type Description object

The value for the longest matching prefix.

Raises:

Type Description KeyError

If no prefix in the trie covers key.

docs.title: __delitem__(key: NetworkKey) -> None
docs.text:
Delete a prefix using del t[prefix].

Requires an exact match. Host bits are zeroed before lookup.

Raises:

Type Description KeyError

If the prefix is not in the trie.

ValueError

If the trie is frozen or the prefix is invalid.

docs.title: __contains__(key: AddressKey) -> bool
docs.text:
Return True if any prefix in the trie covers key (LPM semantics).

Does not raise on invalid or unrecognised keys — returns False instead.

docs.title: __len__() -> int
docs.text: Return the number of prefixes stored in the trie.

docs.title: __iter__() -> Iterator[str]
docs.text: Iterate over all stored prefixes as CIDR strings.

docs.title: get(key: AddressKey, default: object = None) -> object
docs.text:
Longest-prefix-match lookup, returning default on a miss.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default key AddressKey

An IP address or network prefix.

required default object

Value to return when no prefix matches. Defaults to None.

None

Returns:

Type Description object

The matched value, or default.

docs.title: get_many(keys: Sequence[AddressKey], default: object = None) -> list[object]
docs.text:
Look up multiple keys in one call, returning a list of values.

Each key is resolved by longest-prefix match; misses return default. When the trie is frozen, all trie traversals run without the GIL, enabling true parallel use from multiple threads.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default keys Sequence[AddressKey]

A sequence of IP addresses or network prefixes.

required default object

Value to return for each miss. Defaults to None.

None

Returns:

Type Description list[object]

A list of matched values (or default) in the same order as

list[object]

keys.

docs.title: get_key(key: AddressKey) -> str | None
docs.text:
Return the matching prefix for a longest-prefix-match lookup.

Unlike __getitem__, returns the prefix string (e.g. "10.0.0.0/8") rather than the stored value.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default key AddressKey

An IP address or network prefix.

required

Returns:

Type Description str | None

The matched prefix as a CIDR string, or None on a miss.

docs.title: has_key(key: NetworkKey) -> bool
docs.text:
Return True if key is an exact prefix in the trie.

Unlike __contains__, this requires an exact match — no longest-prefix semantics.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default key NetworkKey

A network prefix (string, IPv4Network, or IPv6Network).

required

Returns:

Type Description bool

True if the exact prefix exists, False otherwise.

docs.title: delete(key: NetworkKey) -> None
docs.text:
Delete an exact prefix from the trie.

Equivalent to del t[prefix]. Host bits are zeroed before lookup.

Raises:

Type Description KeyError

If the prefix is not in the trie.

ValueError

If the trie is frozen or the prefix is invalid.

docs.title: keys() -> list[str]
docs.text: Return a list of all stored prefixes as CIDR strings.

docs.title: dump(path: str | os.PathLike[str]) -> None
docs.text:
Serialize this frozen trie to a binary file.

The file can be loaded in another process with Pattrie.load(). The trie must be frozen before calling this method.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default path str | PathLike[str]

Destination file path.

required

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the trie is not frozen or the file cannot be created.

docs.title: load(path: str | os.PathLike[str]) -> Pattrie classmethod
docs.text:
Load a trie from a binary file written by dump().

Uses memory-mapped I/O for efficiency. The returned trie is frozen.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default path str | PathLike[str]

Path to a file written by dump().

required

Returns:

Type Description Pattrie

A new frozen Pattrie instance.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If the file is not a valid pattrie dump or is corrupt.

docs.title: freeze() -> None
docs.text:
Make the trie read-only and enable GIL-free concurrent reads.

After freezing, __getitem__, get, get_many, and __contains__ all release the GIL, allowing true parallel lookups from multiple threads. Any mutation attempt raises ValueError.

Use thaw() to restore mutability.

docs.title: thaw() -> None
docs.text:
Restore mutability after freeze().

Read operations will re-acquire the GIL. Mutations are allowed again.
